articles.title: Trump’s First Address After Chilling ‘Assassination Attempt’ At Washington Hilton
articles.description: In his first major reveal after the shocking incident at the Washington Hilton, Donald Trump addressed the nation with a tense and measured tone. He described the attacker as a “lone wolf,” but stressed that nothing is confirmed as investigators continue examining every possible angle. The statement, delivered in the aftermath of an alleged assassination attempt, carried a heavy sense of uncertainty and urgency. While authorities piece together the motive and connections, the political atmosphere remains charged, with security fears and unanswered questions hanging over Washington.

articles.title: No peace talks under shadow of blockades and threats: Iran's President Pezeshkian to Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif
articles.description: Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian has refused direct talks with the United States. He stated Iran will not negotiate under pressure or threats. The ongoing naval blockade and hostile US actions are the main obstacles. Pezeshkian urged the US to remove these barriers first. This comes amid stalled peace talks and a US naval blockade.

articles.title: Who Is Pearlman? Man On Stage During Shooting Incident As Trump Was Evacuated
articles.description: Pearlman said his goal was to "bring us together" and give the room 25 minutes of laughter and jaw-dropping moments instead of political jabs.

articles.title: Assembly Elections 2026 Live Updates: CM Stalin taking break in Kodaikanal after intense election work
articles.description: Assembly Elections 2026 Live Updates: After an intensive election campaign tour, Tamil Nadu Chief Minister MK Stalin left for a short trip to Kodaikanal in Dindigul district with his family. During his stay, the Chief Minister on Saturday took a walk around the scenic Kodaikanal Lake, a prominent attraction in the hill town. He was seen interacting with tourists and locals, exchanging greetings, and posing for photographs and selfies.

articles.title: US-Iran war: FM Araghchi to head back to Pakistan after Oman visit
articles.description: US-Iran war news: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is set for another visit to Pakistan. This trip follows his engagements in Oman and precedes his travel to Russia. A portion of his delegation has returned to Tehran for crucial consultations. These discussions are focused on matters related to ending the ongoing conflict. The delegation is expected to rejoin Araghchi in Islamabad soon.

articles.title: PM Modi reacts to gunfire scare at White House dinner
articles.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi conveyed relief following a security incident in Washington DC. US President Donald Trump, the First Lady, and the Vice President were reported safe. An armed individual opened fire at a hotel, causing panic. Law enforcement apprehended the suspect. The event has renewed focus on security at high-profile gatherings.

articles.title: 'Violence has no place in democracy' PM Modi reacts to armed man storming Trump event
articles.description: Indian PM Narendra Modi expressed relief over President Trump’s safety after an armed man stormed a Washington DC hotel during a high-profile event

articles.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has finally reacted to the recent security incident in Washington.

articles.title: Assembly Elections 2026 Live Updates: PM Modi To Hold Multiple Rallies In Bengal Today Ahead Of April 29 Polls
articles.description: The day will conclude with a roadshow in Kolkata at around 5:30 pm, expected to draw large crowds as the BJP intensifies its campaign efforts in the state.

articles.title: US-Iran war: Trump says Tehran presented new offers 10 minutes after he cancelled US team's trip
articles.description: President Donald Trump has rejected a new peace proposal from Iran. He stated the offer was not good enough. Trump emphasized that Iran will not possess nuclear weapons. He mentioned that negotiations were halted due to extensive travel and the absence of direct talks with Iran's leader. The US will now communicate via telephone.

articles.title: "Shadow fleet" intercepted: US Navy forces sanctioned tanker Sevan to retreat toward Iran
articles.description: The US Navy has intercepted an Iranian vessel, the M/V Sevan, in the Arabian Sea. This ship is part of Iran's 'shadow fleet' and was forced to return to Iranian waters. This action is part of a broader US naval blockade targeting Iranian energy exports. US forces are enforcing sanctions and have redirected 37 vessels since the blockade began.

articles.title: Defection jolt prompts AAP to close ranks in Punjab
articles.description: AAP's Punjab unit is regrouping after seven Rajya Sabha members defected to BJP, stressing the need for better communication ahead of assembly polls.

articles.title: BJP's Manoj Tiwari slams TMC on women's safety, healthcare in Bengal
articles.description: At a Hooghly rally, BJP's Manoj Tiwari declared Mamata Banerjee's era over. He accused TMC of denying health schemes to the poor and siding with rapists, promising development with a 'double-engine' government in West Bengal.

articles.title: Janivara row: Unacceptable, says Karnataka Health Minister Dinesh Gundu Rao
articles.description: CET exam: Dinesh Gundu Rao condemns forcing students to remove sacred thread and other religious symbols during CET exam in Bengaluru calling incident inhumane, police investigation underway and government assures action to protect student dignity .

articles.title: Ae Maalik Tere Bande Ham
articles.description: A song that asks for no allegiance and seeks no conversion, but simply holds up a mirror to your moral self. A meditation on awareness, perhaps, is exactly what our world needs right now.

articles.title: China launches Pakistani satellite
articles.description: China successfully launched Pakistan's PRSC-EO3 satellite on Saturday night. This marks the fourth Pakistani satellite China has put into orbit since last year. The launch expands China's space cooperation with Pakistan. Additionally, two Pakistani astronauts have arrived in Beijing for astronaut training. One will become the first foreign astronaut on China's Tiangong space station.

articles.title: "Not democracy but TMC's reign of terror": Rahul Gandhi condems killing of Congress worker Debdeep Chatterjee
articles.description: Rahul Gandhi and the West Bengal Congress have strongly condemned the alleged murder of Congress worker Debdeep Chatterjee. They claim TMC-affiliated individuals are responsible for the death. Gandhi demanded immediate action and harsh punishment for the accused. The West Bengal Pradesh Congress Committee called the incident a collapse of law and order and sought justice for the victim's family.

articles.title: “Disrespect from the start”: Marco Trungelliti accuses Madrid Open crowd of abuse as officials face backlash over inaction
articles.description: Marco Trungelliti has accused the Madrid Open crowd of racist abuse and disrespect during his match against Daniel Merida Aguilar. The Argentine said officials did little to stop the behavior. His comments have added to the tournament’s growing list of controversies, including past complaints from Fabio Fognini, Coco Gauff, and Iga Swiatek.

articles.title: ‘I have 3 weeks left in US’: Fired H1-B worker says he is racing against time after job loss

articles.title: Fuming Mamata leaves the stage amidst ‘chor’ slogans from BJP rally in Bhabanipur
articles.description: "Everyone saw what happened. How our chief minister was insulted. This will go against the BJP," said Sovandeb Chatterjee, a senior TMC leader who was present at the public meeting.

articles.title: Mahila Congress attacks BJP over 'stalled' Women's Reservation Bill
articles.description: The HP Mahila Congress slammed the BJP for delaying the Women's Reservation Bill by linking it to Census and delimitation, calling it a 'betrayal'. It demanded immediate implementation and announced a plan to send 1 million postcards to PM Modi.

articles.title: Himachal HC nullifies controversial Act, grants relief to employees
articles.description: The Himachal Pradesh High Court has struck down a state law as "unconstitutional" in a landmark verdict. The ruling quashes the Act, invalidates related orders, and grants sweeping relief to employees in over 260 connected cases.

articles.title: Real fight is with Congress, not TMC: Rahul Gandhi in West Bengal
articles.description: Rahul Gandhi attacked the TMC and BJP in West Bengal, alleging widespread corruption. He asserted that the BJP knows the real political fight is with the Congress, not the TMC, and accused the saffron party of stealing votes in other states.

articles.title: Former CJI Chandrachud calls for regulation of harmful online content
articles.description: Former CJI DY Chandrachud called for legislation to regulate online content, stressing the need to protect children and women from gender violence. He highlighted the easy availability of harmful material and urged for policy-level intervention.

articles.title: FIR against Pappu Yadav for 'insulting' remarks on women in politics
articles.description: JDU leader Rina Choudhary filed an FIR against Purnea MP Pappu Yadav for his remark that "90% of women cannot do politics without entering the room of politicians". Choudhary called the statement "deeply insulting" and demanded a public apology.

articles.title: Viksit Bharat by 2047: Students' key role stressed by Vijender Gupta
articles.description: Delhi Assembly Speaker Vijender Gupta urged students at PGDAV College to help build a 'Viksit Bharat' by 2047. He stressed that education alone is not enough; it must be complemented by strong character and values for national progress.

articles.title: Residents stage protest, seek house sites in Mangalagiri
articles.description: Residents in Mangalagiri protest for house sites, construction aid, and ownership pattas, demanding action from local authorities.

articles.title: Who Is Matthew James Sullivan? Would-be UFO Whistleblower Dies Ahead of Congress Testimony
articles.description: Matthew James Sullivan's death has raised alarms among lawmakers, prompting Eric Burlison to refer the case to the FBI due to potential national security implications.

articles.title: Sukhbir Badal rebuilds SAD quietly in Punjab
articles.description: Sukhbir Badal is actively working to strengthen the Akali Dal for upcoming Punjab elections. He is focusing on direct interactions and small gatherings rather than large rallies. The party has seen a positive response from people following their flood relief efforts. An alliance with the BJP is also being considered.

articles.description: The US has also blockaded Iran's ports, seized ships tied to Tehran and planned to take part in a second round of ceasefire talks in Pakistan this weekend

articles.title: Sleep on a yoga mat, work without pay: Singapore domestic helper’s shocking claim against British-Indian family goes viral
articles.description: A domestic worker in Singapore alleged her new British-Indian employers made her sleep on a yoga mat and refused to pay for four days of work. She also claimed her work permit was not processed, preventing her from legally working. The story gained traction online, prompting calls for reporting the incident to the Ministry of Manpower.

articles.title: FAPTO steps up protest seeking resolution of pending demands
articles.description: FAPTO intensifies protests with a relay hunger strike, demanding resolution of 17 key educational and financial issues.
